data.content: I can see you have an MSTest project with a failing test — the express shipping test expects `15.00m` but the method returns `10.00m`. Let me start `dotnet watch test` so you get automatic re-runs on every file save:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use `dotnet watch` to set up hot reload for their tests. This is an MSTest project targeting .NET 8.0. I can see that the express test is failing because `CalculateShipping("express", 1.0)` returns `10.00m` but the test expects `15.00m`.

Let me set up `dotnet watch test` for them so they can iterate with hot reload.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-13b3b36d51424256ada18907037e2ec2 && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test with hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

toolArgs.description: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload
